notes: Derived from the previously working Fruit Jam USB NSH/Berry profile; the reduced profile requires clean-build and board smoke validation.

profiles.summary: USB CDC NSH, Berry, vi, readline/history, Ctrl+C, I2C and normal-reset watchdog.

notes: Seeded from the prior Pico 2 W USB console profile; Berry, vi, I2C and watchdog additions require validation.

profiles.summary: USB CDC NSH, Berry, vi, readline/history, Ctrl+C, I2C and normal-reset watchdog; CYW43439 disabled.

notes: 2026-07-12: Fruit Jam hardware passed the 16-check network service audit, mixed 2 MiB FTP plus 200-request HTTP stress, ping, wget, NTP, and netcat with zero transport, driver, or TCP allocation failures.

profiles.summary: Minimal profile plus compile-time-selectable SPI or PIO microSD at /mnt/sd0, ESP-Hosted wlan0, DHCP/DNS, FTP, HTTP, ping, NTP, netcat, telnet, wget and WAPI utilities.

notes: CYW43439 GSPI association, DHCP and sustained traffic remain live-hardware validation items; no SSID or passphrase is embedded.

profiles.summary: Minimal profile plus CYW43439 GSPI, DHCP/DNS, FTP, HTTP, ping, NTP, netcat, telnet, wget and WAPI utilities.

notes: The source profile previously worked on hardware; the thin-wrapper migration still requires regression validation.

profiles.summary: Autostart TRMNL client with ESP-Hosted, PSRAM, 800x480 Y2 internal DVI scanout and storage fallback.

notes: NXDoom keyboard input was previously working.
USB mouse device support and I2S audio are included, but NXDoom mouse mapping and sound backend remain implementation and hardware-validation work.

profiles.summary: Focused NXDoom image with minimum tools, PSRAM, RGB565 DVI, SD /data, USB keyboard/mouse devices and I2S audio devices.

notes: The constituent Fruit Jam drivers previously worked in broader images; the independent full profile requires matrix regression testing.

profiles.summary: Minimum and network features plus PSRAM, SD, DVI, audio, NeoPixels, IR RC, buttons, I2C/SPI, USB HID/hub/composite/Xbox, LVGL and diagnostics.

notes: FruitClaw owns /dev/watchdog0 through its session guard in this profile; the generic watchdog service is deliberately disabled.
Autostart and external secret-file paths require post-migration regression testing.

profiles.summary: Exact full hardware baseline plus FruitClaw boot autostart and normal-reset FruitClaw session watchdog.
